---
title: "Llama: Meta's Open-Weight Large Language Model"
author:
  - name: ML+X Community

date: 2026-02-24
date-format: long
image: "../../../images/vincent-van-zalinge-mDohQISBnCk-unsplash.jpg"

categories:
  - Models
  - LLM
  - NLP
  - Deep learning
  - Transformers
  - Foundation models
---
## About this resource

[Llama](https://www.llama.com/) is a family of open-weight large language models developed by Meta AI. First released in February 2023, the Llama series has become the most widely adopted open-weight LLM family, enabling researchers and developers to run, fine-tune, and build upon powerful language models without relying on proprietary APIs. Llama models are trained on large-scale public datasets and are available in multiple sizes to accommodate different hardware and use-case requirements.

#### Key features
- **Architecture**: Decoder-only transformer with improvements including grouped-query attention (GQA), RoPE positional embeddings, and SwiGLU activations.
- **Open weights**: Model weights are freely available for research and commercial use (under the Llama Community License), enabling full customization and local deployment.
- **Multiple sizes**: Available in various parameter counts (e.g., 1B, 3B, 8B, 70B, 405B for Llama 3), allowing users to choose the right trade-off between capability and hardware requirements.

## Timeline context
Llama fits into the rapid development of open-weight language models:

- **[GPT-2 (2019)](https://openai.com/index/better-language-models/)**: OpenAI's 1.5B parameter model, one of the first widely discussed open LLMs.
- **[Llama (2023)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2302.13971)**: Meta's first open-weight LLM release, demonstrating that smaller, well-trained models can match larger ones.
- **[Llama 2 (2023)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2307.09288)**: Added chat-tuned variants and an open commercial license.
- **[Llama 3 / 3.1 / 3.2 (2024)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2407.21783)**: Significant quality improvements, expanded context length (128K tokens), and multimodal capabilities in 3.2.

## Llama variants
- **Llama 3.2 (1B, 3B)**: Lightweight models suitable for on-device and edge deployment.
- **Llama 3.1 (8B, 70B, 405B)**: The flagship model family with strong performance across reasoning, coding, and multilingual tasks.
- **Llama 3.2 Vision (11B, 90B)**: Multimodal variants that can process both text and images.
- **Code Llama**: Specialized for code generation and understanding.

## Model playground
#### Tutorials and getting started
- **[Hugging Face model page](https://huggingface.co/meta-llama)**: Download model weights and find usage examples.
- **[Ollama](https://ollama.com/)**: The simplest way to run Llama locally — just `ollama run llama3.2`.
- **[Llama Recipes](https://github.com/meta-llama/llama-recipes)**: Official collection of examples for fine-tuning, inference, and deployment.

#### High-level tips for effective use
- **Quantization**: Use quantized versions (e.g., 4-bit via GPTQ or GGUF) to run larger models on consumer hardware.
- **Fine-tuning**: Use parameter-efficient methods like LoRA (via Hugging Face PEFT) to adapt Llama to domain-specific tasks without requiring massive compute.
- **System prompts**: For chat-tuned variants, well-crafted system prompts significantly improve output quality and consistency.

#### Related datasets & challenges
- [**Open LLM Leaderboard**](https://huggingface.co/spaces/open-llm-leaderboard/open_llm_leaderboard): Benchmark comparing open-weight LLMs across standard evaluation tasks.

## Questions?
If you have any lingering questions about this resource, please feel free to post to the [Nexus Q&A](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/ML-X-Nexus/discussions/categories/q-a) on GitHub. We will improve materials on this website as additional questions come in.

## See also
- [**Library**: Hugging Face Transformers](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Libraries/HuggingFace-Transformers.html): The primary library for loading and running Llama models in Python.
- [**Tool**: Ollama](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/GenAI/Ollama.html): Run Llama and other open-weight LLMs locally with a single command.
- [**Model**: Whisper (OpenAI)](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Models/Whisper.html): Another prominent open-weight model, focused on speech recognition.
